name: fixture-06-banker-commercial-lending-vp
description: VP of Commercial Lending at a regional bank applying to a middle-market corporate banking role at a super-regional. Direct fit on lending fundamentals; stretch is larger hold sizes + syndication desk coordination.

resume_data.name: James O'Halloran
key_achievements: Grew commercial loan book from $210M to $380M over 3 years with NCO consistently below 8bps across the cycle.
Originated 14 new C&I relationships averaging $12M each — client acquisition in a mature, competitive regional market.
Chaired the regional credit committee that approved a $340M portfolio of new originations in FY 2024; zero criticized credits emerged from that vintage through the stress window.

jd_analysis.role_title: Senior Vice President, Middle Market Corporate Banking
requirements: 10+ years of C&I lending or corporate banking experience
Demonstrated track record originating and structuring credits in the $25M-$100M range
Experience coordinating with syndication and capital markets desks on larger holds
Strong command of financial statement analysis and covenant structuring
Executive presence with C-suite and private equity sponsor clients
culture_cues: relationship-first — transactional bankers don't last here
partnership with wealth management and treasury on client coverage

letter_plan.opening_hook: Lead with the NCO-below-8bps portfolio performance — demonstrates credit discipline is the default instinct — before getting into the hold-size step-up the JD implies.
body_points: $210M → $380M growth + clean cycle performance is the credit-discipline-above-volume proof Atlantic wants; the 14 new C&I relationships average $12M — acknowledge this is below the JD's $25M-$100M range and position it as the hold-size progression the role offers, not a gap.

Originations in a mature market demonstrate the relationship-first muscle — competitive markets reward persistent, trust-based coverage, not transactional outreach. This maps directly to Atlantic's stated culture.

Chairing the regional credit committee proves the credit-structuring command + committee presentation muscle; mention the zero-criticized-credits outcome through stress as evidence of structuring instinct under volatility.
letter_plan.closing_strategy: Address the syndication coordination gap honestly — regional-bank holds rarely require capital markets partnership, so that's a learning curve. Close on the cross-functional coverage muscle (wealth + treasury partnership already modeled at current bank) as cultural-fit proof.
